title: Privacy-preserving lookup/search (client-side index; no query logging; no precise-geolocation storage)
context: A water-system query reveals a person's location and a potential health concern. These queries are treated as sensitive and must never be logged server-side, stored, or sent to a third party. The lookup must function via a client-side index where feasible — so there is no server to log the query — and must work without collecting or storing the user's precise geolocation. The runtime egress E2E test from wqe-web-006 must extend to all lookup flows.
objective: Implement privacy-preserving water-system lookup/search in the web app: (1) client-side index or pre-rendered lookup so no query reaches a server; (2) no server-side query log (architecturally impossible with static hosting); (3) no third-party calls during lookup — verified by extending the runtime network-interception E2E to cover all lookup flows; (4) precise user geolocation is never stored or transmitted; (5) lookup is accessible: keyboard-navigable, screen-reader labeled, meets WCAG 2.2 AA contrast; (6) zero critical axe violations on lookup pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Lookup works with no server-side query log; queries do not leave the client during normal operation
No third-party calls during any lookup flow — verified by runtime network-interception E2E test covering all lookup interactions
Precise user geolocation is never stored, transmitted, or logged
Lookup is keyboard-navigable and screen-reader labeled; WCAG 2.2 AA contrast on all lookup elements
Zero critical axe violations on lookup and search pages
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: PR adding client-side search/lookup to the web app with runtime egress E2E test covering all lookup flows and a11y verification on lookup pages.
